hcl.DiagError,\n185: \t\t\tSummary:  `Invalid \"count\" attribute`,\n186: \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The \"count\" object does not have an attribute named %q. The only supported attribute is count.index, which is the index of each instance of a resource block that has the \"count\" argument set.`, addr.Name),\n187: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n188: \t\t})\n189: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n190: \t}\n191: }\n192: \n193: func (d *evaluationStateData) GetForEachAttr(addr addrs.ForEachAttr, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n194: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n195: \tvar returnVal cty.Value\n196: \tswitch addr.Name {\n197: \n198: \tcase \"key\":\n199: \t\treturnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachKey\n200: \tcase \"value\":\n201: \t\treturnVal = d.InstanceKeyData.EachValue\n202: \n203: \t\tif returnVal == cty.NilVal {\n204: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n205: \t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n206: \t\t\t\tSummary:  `each.value cannot be used in this context`,\n207: \t\t\t\tDetail:   `A reference to \"each.value\" has been used in a context in which it is unavailable, such as when the configuration no longer contains the value in its \"for_each\" expression. Remove this reference to each.value in your configuration to work around this error.`,\n208: \t\t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n209: \t\t\t})\n210: \t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags\n211: \t\t}\n212: \tdefault:\n213: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n214: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n215: \t\t\tSummary:  `Invalid \"each\" attribute`,\n216: \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The \"each\" object does not have an attribute named %q. The supported attributes are each.key and each.value, the current key and value pair of the \"for_each\" attribute set.`, addr.Name),\n217: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n218: \t\t})\n219: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n220: \t}\n221: \n222: \tif returnVal == cty.NilVal {\n223: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n224: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n225: \t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to \"each\" in context without for_each`,\n226: \t\t\tDetail:   `The \"each\" object can be used only in \"module\" or \"resource\" blocks, and only when the \"for_each\" argument is set.`,\n227: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n228: \t\t})\n229: \t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cty.DynamicPseudoType), diags\n230: \t}\n231: \treturn returnVal, diags\n232: }\n233: \n234: func (d *evaluationStateData) GetInputVariable(addr addrs.InputVariable, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n235: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n236: \n237: \t// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,\n238: \t// so we can produce a nice message if not.\n239: \tmoduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)\n240: \tif moduleConfig == nil {\n241: \t\t// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module\n242: \t\t// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.\n243: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"input variable read from %s, which has no configuration\", d.ModulePath))\n244: \t}\n245: \n246: \tconfig := moduleConfig.Module.Variables[addr.Name]\n247: \tif config == nil {\n248: \t\tvar suggestions []string\n249: \t\tfor k := range moduleConfig.Module.Variables {\n250: \t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions, k)\n251: \t\t}\n252: \t\tsuggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)\n253: \t\tif suggestion != \"\" {\n254: \t\t\tsuggestion = fmt.Sprintf(\" Did you mean %q?\", suggestion)\n255: \t\t} else {\n256: \t\t\tsuggestion = fmt.Sprintf(\" This variable can be declared with a variable %q {} block.\", addr.Name)\n257: \t\t}\n258: \n259: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n260: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n261: \t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to undeclared input variable`,\n262: \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An input variable with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),\n263: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n264: \t\t})\n265: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n266: \t}\n267: \n268: \t// During the validate walk, input variables are always unknown so\n269: \t// that we are validating the configuration for all possible input values\n270: \t// rather than for a specific set. Checking against a specific set of\n271: \t// input values then happens during the plan walk.\n272: \t//\n273: \t// This is important because otherwise the validation walk will tend to be\n274: \t// overly strict, requiring expressions throughout the configuration to\n275: \t// be complicated to accommodate all possible inputs, whereas returning\n276: \t// unknown here allows for simpler patterns like using input values as\n277: \t// guards to broadly enable/disable resources, avoid processing things\n278: \t// that are disabled, etc. Terraform's static validation leans towards\n279: \t// being liberal in what it accepts because the subsequent plan walk has\n280: \t// more information available and so can be more conservative.\n281: \tif d.Operation == walkValidate {\n282: \t\t// We should still capture the statically-configured marks during\n283: \t\t// the validate walk.\n284: \t\tret := cty.UnknownVal(config.Type)\n285: \t\tif config.Sensitive {\n286: \t\t\tret = ret.Mark(marks.Sensitive)\n287: \t\t}\n288: \t\tif config.Ephemeral {\n289: \t\t\tret = ret.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\n290: \t\t}\n291: \t\treturn ret, diags\n292: \t}\n293: \n294: \tval := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetInputVariableValue(d.ModulePath.InputVariable(addr.Name))\n295: \n296: \t// Mark if sensitive and/or ephemeral\n297: \tif config.Sensitive {\n298: \t\tval = val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)\n299: \t}\n300: \tif config.Ephemeral {\n301: \t\tval = val.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\n302: \t}\n303: \n304: \treturn val, diags\n305: }\n306: \n307: func (d *evaluationStateData) GetLocalValue(addr addrs.LocalValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n308: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n309: \n310: \t// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,\n311: \t// so we can produce a nice message if not.\n312: \tmoduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)\n313: \tif moduleConfig == nil {\n314: \t\t// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module\n315: \t\t// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.\n316: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"local value read from %s, which has no configuration\", d.ModulePath))\n317: \t}\n318: \n319: \tconfig := moduleConfig.Module.Locals[addr.Name]\n320: \tif config == nil {\n321: \t\tvar suggestions []string\n322: \t\tfor k := range moduleConfig.Module.Locals {\n323: \t\t\tsuggestions = append(suggestions, k)\n324: \t\t}\n325: \t\tsuggestion := didyoumean.NameSuggestion(addr.Name, suggestions)\n326: \t\tif suggestion != \"\" {\n327: \t\t\tsuggestion = fmt.Sprintf(\" Did you mean %q?\", suggestion)\n328: \t\t}\n329: \n330: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n331: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n332: \t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to undeclared local value`,\n333: \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A local value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),\n334: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n335: \t\t})\n336: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n337: \t}\n338: \n339: \tval := d.Evaluator.NamedValues.GetLocalValue(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))\n340: \treturn val, diags\n341: }\n342: \n343: func (d *evaluationStateData) GetModule(addr addrs.ModuleCall, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n344: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n345: \n346: \t// Output results live in the module that declares them, which is one of\n347: \t// the child module instances of our current module path.\n348: \tmoduleAddr := d.ModulePath.Module().Child(addr.Name)\n349: \tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)\n350: \n351: \tparentCfg := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)\n352: \tcallConfig, ok := parentCfg.Module.ModuleCalls[addr.Name]\n353: \tif !ok {\n354: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n355: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n356: \t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to undeclared module`,\n357: \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The configuration contains no %s.`, moduleAddr),\n358: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n359: \t\t})\n360: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n361: \t}\n362: \n363: \t// We'll consult the configuration to see what output names we are\n364: \t// expecting, so we can ensure the resulting object is of the expected\n365: \t// type even if our data is incomplete for some reason.\n366: \tmoduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.Descendant(moduleAddr)\n367: \tif moduleConfig == nil {\n368: \t\t// should never happen, since we have a valid module call above, this\n369: \t\t// should be caught during static validation.\n370: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"output value read from %s, which has no configuration\", moduleAddr))\n371: \t}\n372: \toutputConfigs := moduleConfig.Module.Outputs\n373: \n374: \t// We don't do instance expansion during validation, and so we need to\n375: \t// return an unknown value. Technically we should always return\n376: \t// cty.DynamicVal here because the final value during plan will always\n377: \t// be an object or tuple type with unpredictable attributes/elements,\n378: \t// but because we never actually carry values forward from validation to\n379: \t// planning we lie a little here and return unknown list and map types,\n380: \t// just to give us more opportunities to catch author mistakes during\n381: \t// validation.\n382: \t//\n383: \t// This means that in practice any expression that refers to a module\n384: \t// call must be written to be valid for either a collection type or\n385: \t// structural type of similar kind, so that it can be considered as\n386: \t// valid during both the validate and plan walks.\n387: \tif d.Operation == walkValidate {\n388: \t\tatys := make(map[string]cty.Type, len(outputConfigs))\n389: \t\tfor name := range outputConfigs {\n390: \t\t\tatys[name] = cty.DynamicPseudoType // output values are dynamically-typed\n391: \t\t}\n392: \t\tinstTy := cty.Object(atys)\n393: \n394: \t\tswitch {\n395: \t\tcase callConfig.Count != nil:\n396: \t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cty.List(instTy)), diags\n397: \t\tcase callConfig.ForEach != nil:\n398: \t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cty.Map(instTy)), diags\n399: \t\tdefault:\n400: \t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(instTy), diags\n401: \t\t}\n402: \t}\n403: \n404: \t// For all other walk types, we proceed to dynamic evaluation of individual\n405: \t// instances, using the global instance expander. An earlier graph node\n406: \t// should always have registered the expansion of this module call before\n407: \t// we get here, unless there's a bug in the graph builders.\n408: \tallInstances := d.Evaluator.Instances\n409: \tinstKeyType, instKeys, known := allInstances.ExpandAbsModuleCall(absAddr)\n410: \tif !known {\n411: \t\t// If we don't know which instances exist then we can't really predict\n412: \t\t// anything at all. We can't even predict the return type based on\n413: \t\t// instKeyType because output values are dynamically-typed and so\n414: \t\t// our final result will always be an object or tuple type whose\n415: \t\t// attribute/element count we cannot predict.\n416: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n417: \t}\n418: \n419: \tinstanceObjVal := func(instKey addrs.InstanceKey) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n420: \t\t// This function must always return a valid value, even if it's\n421: \t\t// just a cty.DynamicVal placeholder accompanying error diagnostics.\n422: \t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n423: \n424: \t\tnamedVals := d.Evaluator.NamedValues\n425: \t\tmoduleInstAddr := absAddr.Instance(instKey)\n426: \t\tattrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(outputConfigs))\n427: \t\tfor name := range outputConfigs {\n428: \t\t\toutputAddr := moduleInstAddr.OutputValue(name)\n429: \n430: \t\t\t// Although we do typically expect the graph dependencies to\n431: \t\t\t// ensure that values get registered before they are needed,\n432: \t\t\t// we track depedencies with specific output values where\n433: \t\t\t// possible, instead of with entire module calls, and so\n434: \t\t\t// in this specific case it's valid for some of this call's\n435: \t\t\t// output values to not be known yet, with the graph builder\n436: \t\t\t// being responsible for making sure that no expression\n437: \t\t\t// in the configuration can actually observe that.\n438: \t\t\tif !namedVals.HasOutputValue(outputAddr) {\n439: \t\t\t\tattrs[name] = cty.DynamicVal\n440: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n441: \t\t\t}\n442: \t\t\toutputVal := namedVals.GetOutputValue(outputAddr)\n443: \t\t\tattrs[name] = outputVal\n444: \t\t}\n445: \n446: \t\treturn cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags\n447: \t}\n448: \n449: \tswitch instKeyType {\n450: \n451: \tcase addrs.NoKeyType:\n452: \t\t// In this case we should always have exactly one instance that\n453: \t\t// is addrs.NoKey. If not then there's a bug in the [instances.Expander]\n454: \t\t// implementation.\n455: \t\tif len(instKeys) != 1 {\n456: \t\t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"module call has no instance key type but has %d instances (should be 1)\", len(instKeys)))\n457: \t\t}\n458: \t\tret, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKeys[0])\n459: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n460: \t\treturn ret, diags\n461: \n462: \tcase addrs.IntKeyType:\n463: \t\t// We can assume that the instance keys are in ascending numerical order\n464: \t\t// and are consecutive, per the contract of allInstances.ExpandModuleCall.\n465: \t\telems := make([]cty.Value, 0, len(instKeys))\n466: \t\tfor _, instKey := range instKeys {\n467: \t\t\tinstVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)\n468: \t\t\telems = append(elems, instVal)\n469: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n470: \t\t}\n471: \t\treturn cty.TupleVal(elems), diags\n472: \n473: \tcase addrs.StringKeyType:\n474: \t\tattrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(instKeys))\n475: \t\tfor _, instKey := range instKeys {\n476: \t\t\tinstVal, moreDiags := instanceObjVal(instKey)\n477: \t\t\tattrs[string(instKey.(addrs.StringKey))] = instVal\n478: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n479: \t\t}\n480: \t\treturn cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags\n481: \n482: \tdefault:\n483: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n484: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n485: \t\t\tSummary:  `Unsupported instance key type`,\n486: \t\t\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\n487: \t\t\t\t`Module call %s has instance key type %#v, which is not supported by the expression evaluator. This is a bug in Terraform.`,\n488: \t\t\t\tabsAddr, instKeyType,\n489: \t\t\t),\n490: \t\t\tSubject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n491: \t\t})\n492: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n493: \t}\n494: }\n495: \n496: func (d *evaluationStateData) GetResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n497: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n498: \t// First we'll consult the configuration to see if an resource of this\n499: \t// name is declared at all.\n500: \tmoduleAddr := d.ModulePath\n501: \tmoduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(moduleAddr)\n502: \tif moduleConfig == nil {\n503: \t\t// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module\n504: \t\t// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.\n505: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"resource value read from %s, which has no configuration\", moduleAddr))\n506: \t}\n507: \n508: \tconfig := moduleConfig.Module.ResourceByAddr(addr)\n509: \tif config == nil {\n510: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n511: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n512: \t\t\tSummary:  `Reference to undeclared resource`,\n513: \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`A resource %q %q has not been declared in %s`, addr.Type, addr.Name, moduleDisplayAddr(moduleAddr)),\n514: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n515: \t\t})\n516: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n517: \t}\n518: \n519: \t// Much of this function was written before we had factored out the handling\n520: \t// of instance keys into the separate instance expander model, and so it\n521: \t// does a bunch of instance-related work itself below.\n522: \t//\n523: \t// Currently, unknown instance keys are only possible when planning with\n524: \t// DeferralAllowed set to true in the PlanOpts, which should only be the\n525: \t// case in the stacks runtime (not the \"normal terraform\" modules runtime).\n526: \t// Thus, we have some amount of duplicated code remaining, to be more\n527: \t// certain that stacks-specific behaviors won't leak out into the standard\n528: \t// runtime.\n529: \t//\n530: \t// TODO: When deferred actions are more stable and robust in stacks, it\n531: \t// would be nice to rework this function to rely on the ResourceInstanceKeys\n532: \t// result for _all_ of its work, rather than continuing to duplicate a bunch\n533: \t// of the logic we've tried to encapsulate over ther already.\n534: \tif d.Operation == walkPlan || d.Operation == walkApply {\n535: \t\tif _, _, hasUnknownKeys := d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys(addr.Absolute(moduleAddr)); hasUnknownKeys {\n536: \t\t\t// There really isn't anything interesting we can do in this situation,\n537: \t\t\t// because it means we have an unknown for_each/count, in which case\n538: \t\t\t// we can't even predict what the result type will be because it\n539: \t\t\t// would be either an object or tuple type decided based on the instance\n540: \t\t\t// keys.\n541: \t\t\t// (We can't get in here for a single-instance resource because in that\n542: \t\t\t// case we would know that there's only one key and it's addrs.NoKey,\n543: \t\t\t// so we'll fall through to the other logic below.)\n544: \t\t\tunknownVal := cty.DynamicVal\n545: \n546: \t\t\t// If an ephemeral resource is deferred we need to mark the returned unknown value as ephemeral\n547: \t\t\tif addr.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n548: \t\t\t\tunknownVal = unknownVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\n549: \t\t\t}\n550: \t\t\treturn unknownVal, diags\n551: \t\t}\n552: \t}\n553: \n554: \t// Build the provider address from configuration, since we may not have\n555: \t// state available in all cases.\n556: \t// We need to build an abs provider address, but we can use a default\n557: \t// instance since we're only interested in the schema.\n558: \tschema := d.getResourceSchema(addr, config.Provider)\n559: \tif schema == nil {\n560: \t\t// This shouldn't happen, since validation before we get here should've\n561: \t\t// taken care of it, but we'll show a reasonable error message anyway.\n562: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n563: \t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n564: \t\t\tSummary:  `Missing resource type schema`,\n565: \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"No schema is available for %s in %s. This is a bug in Terraform and should be reported.\", addr, config.Provider),\n566: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n567: \t\t})\n568: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n569: \t}\n570: \tty := schema.ImpliedType()\n571: \n572: \tif addr.Mode == addrs.EphemeralResourceMode {\n573: \t\t// FIXME: This does not yet work with deferrals, and it would be nice to\n574: \t\t// find some way to refactor this so that the following code is not so\n575: \t\t// tethered to the current implementation details. Instead we should\n576: \t\t// have an abstract idea of first determining what instances the\n577: \t\t// resource has (using d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys) and\n578: \t\t// then retrieving the value for each instance to assemble into the\n579: \t\t// result, using some per-resource-mode logic maintained elsewhere.\n580: \t\treturn d.getEphemeralResource(addr, rng)\n581: \t}\n582: \n583: \trs := d.Evaluator.State.Resource(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath))\n584: \n585: \tif rs == nil {\n586: \t\tswitch d.Operation {\n587: \t\tcase walkPlan, walkApply:\n588: \t\t\t// During plan and apply as we evaluate each removed instance they\n589: \t\t\t// are removed from the working state. Since we know there are no\n590: \t\t\t// instances, return an empty container of the expected type.\n591: \t\t\tswitch {\n592: \t\t\tcase config.Count != nil:\n593: \t\t\t\treturn cty.EmptyTupleVal, diags\n594: \t\t\tcase config.ForEach != nil:\n595: \t\t\t\treturn cty.EmptyObjectVal, diags\n596: \t\t\tdefault:\n597: \t\t\t\t// While we can reference an expanded resource with 0\n598: \t\t\t\t// instances, we cannot reference instances that do not exist.\n599: \t\t\t\t// Due to the fact that we may have direct references to\n600: \t\t\t\t// instances that may end up in a root output during destroy\n601: \t\t\t\t// (since a planned destroy cannot yet remove root outputs), we\n602: \t\t\t\t// need to return a dynamic value here to allow evaluation to\n603: \t\t\t\t// continue.\n604: \t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[ERROR] unknown instance %q referenced during %s\", addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath), d.Operation)\n605: \t\t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n606: \t\t\t}\n607: \n608: \t\tcase walkImport:\n609: \t\t\t// Import does not yet plan resource changes, so new resources from\n610: \t\t\t// config are not going to be found here. Once walkImport fully\n611: \t\t\t// plans resources, this case should not longer be needed.\n612: \t\t\t// In the single instance case, we can return a typed unknown value\n613: \t\t\t// for the instance to better satisfy other expressions using the\n614: \t\t\t// value. This of course will not help if statically known\n615: \t\t\t// attributes are expected to be known elsewhere, but reduces the\n616: \t\t\t// number of problematic configs for now.\n617: \t\t\t// Unlike in plan and apply above we can't be sure the count or\n618: \t\t\t// for_each instances are empty, so we return a DynamicVal. We\n619: \t\t\t// don't really have a good value to return otherwise -- empty\n620: \t\t\t// values will fail for direct index expressions, and unknown\n621: \t\t\t// Lists and Maps could fail in some type unifications.\n622: \t\t\tswitch {\n623: \t\t\tcase config.Count != nil:\n624: \t\t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n625: \t\t\tcase config.ForEach != nil:\n626: \t\t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n627: \t\t\tdefault:\n628: \t\t\t\treturn cty.UnknownVal(ty), diags\n629: \t\t\t}\n630: \n631: \t\tdefault:\n632: \t\t\t// We should only end up here during the validate walk (or\n633: \t\t\t// console/eval), since later walks should have at least partial\n634: \t\t\t// states populated for all resources in the configuration.\n635: \t\t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, diags\n636: \t\t}\n637: \t}\n638: \n639: \t// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource\n640: \t// or resources that are in the state.\n641: \tinstances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}\n642: \n643: \t// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the\n644: \t// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be\n645: \t// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.\n646: \tfor key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {\n647: \t\tinstances[key] = value\n648: \t}\n649: \n650: \t// Decode all instances in the current state\n651: \tpendingDestroy := d.Operation == walkDestroy\n652: \tfor key, is := range rs.Instances {\n653: \t\tif _, ok := instances[key]; ok {\n654: \t\t\t// Then we've already loaded this instance from the deferrals so\n655: \t\t\t// we'll just ignore it being in state.\n656: \t\t\tcontinue\n657: \t\t}\n658: \t\t// Otherwise, we'll load the instance from state.\n659: \n660: \t\tif is == nil || is.Current == nil {\n661: \t\t\t// Assume we're dealing with an instance that hasn't been created yet.\n662: \t\t\tinstances[key] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)\n663: \t\t\tcontinue\n664: \t\t}\n665: \n666: \t\tinstAddr := addr.Instance(key).Absolute(d.ModulePath)\n667: \t\tchange := d.Evaluator.Changes.GetResourceInstanceChange(instAddr, addrs.NotDeposed)\n668: \t\tif change != nil {\n669: \t\t\t// Don't take any resources that are yet to be deleted into account.\n670: \t\t\t// If the referenced resource is CreateBeforeDestroy, then orphaned\n671: \t\t\t// instances will be in the state, as they are not destroyed until\n672: \t\t\t// after their dependants are updated.\n673: \t\t\tif change.Action == plans.Delete {\n674: \t\t\t\tif !pendingDestroy {\n675: \t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n676: \t\t\t\t}\n677: \t\t\t}\n678: \t\t}\n679: \n680: \t\t// Planned resources are temporarily stored in state with empty values,\n681: \t\t// and need to be replaced by the planned value here.\n682: \t\tif is.Current.Status == states.ObjectPlanned {\n683: \t\t\tif change == nil {\n684: \t\t\t\t// FIXME: This is usually an unfortunate case where we need to\n685: \t\t\t\t// lookup an individual instance referenced via \"self\" for\n686: \t\t\t\t// postconditions which we know exists, but because evaluation\n687: \t\t\t\t// must always get the resource in aggregate some instance\n688: \t\t\t\t// changes may not yet be registered.\n689: \t\t\t\tinstances[key] = cty.DynamicVal\n690: \t\t\t\t// log the problem for debugging, since it may be a legitimate error we can't catch\n691: \t\t\t\tlog.Printf(\"[WARN] instance %s is marked as having a change pending but that change is not recorded in the plan\", instAddr)\n692: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n693: \t\t\t}\n694: \t\t\tinstances[key] = change.After\n695: \t\t\tcontinue\n696: \t\t}\n697: \n698: \t\tios, err := is.Current.Decode(ty)\n699: \t\tif err != nil {\n700: \t\t\t// This shouldn't happen, since by the time we get here we\n701: \t\t\t// should have upgraded the state data already.\n702: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n703: \t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n704: \t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Invalid resource instance data in state\",\n705: \t\t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(\"Instance %s data could not be decoded from the state: %s.\", instAddr, err),\n706: \t\t\t\tSubject:  &config.DeclRange,\n707: \t\t\t})\n708: \t\t\tcontinue\n709: \t\t}\n710: \n711: \t\tval := ios.Value\n712: \n713: \t\tinstances[key] = val\n714: \t}\n715: \n716: \t// ret should be populated with a valid value in all cases below\n717: \tvar ret cty.Value\n718: \n719: \tswitch {\n720: \tcase config.Count != nil:\n721: \t\t// figure out what the last index we have is\n722: \t\tlength := -1\n723: \t\tfor key := range instances {\n724: \t\t\tintKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)\n725: \t\t\tif !ok {\n726: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n727: \t\t\t}\n728: \t\t\tif int(intKey) >= length {\n729: \t\t\t\tlength = int(intKey) + 1\n730: \t\t\t}\n731: \t\t}\n732: \n733: \t\tif length > 0 {\n734: \t\t\tvals := make([]cty.Value, length)\n735: \t\t\tfor key, instance := range instances {\n736: \t\t\t\tintKey, ok := key.(addrs.IntKey)\n737: \t\t\t\tif !ok {\n738: \t\t\t\t\t// old key from state, which isn't valid for evaluation\n739: \t\t\t\t\tcontinue\n740: \t\t\t\t}\n741: \n742: \t\t\t\tvals[int(intKey)] = instance\n743: \t\t\t}\n744: \n745: \t\t\t// Insert unknown values where there are any missing instances\n746: \t\t\tfor i, v := range vals {\n747: \t\t\t\tif v == cty.NilVal {\n748: \t\t\t\t\tvals[i] = cty.UnknownVal(ty)\n749: \t\t\t\t}\n750: \t\t\t}\n751: \t\t\tret = cty.TupleVal(vals)\n752: \t\t} else {\n753: \t\t\tret = cty.EmptyTupleVal\n754: \t\t}\n755: \n756: \tcase config.ForEach != nil:\n757: \t\tvals := make(map[string]cty.Value)\n758: \t\tfor key, instance := range instances {\n759: \t\t\tstrKey, ok := key.(addrs.StringKey)\n760: \t\t\tif !ok {\n761: \t\t\t\t// old key that is being dropped and not used for evaluation\n762: \t\t\t\tcontinue\n763: \t\t\t}\n764: \t\t\tvals[string(strKey)] = instance\n765: \t\t}\n766: \n767: \t\tif len(vals) > 0 {\n768: \t\t\t// We use an object rather than a map here because resource schemas\n769: \t\t\t// may include dynamically-typed attributes, which will then cause\n770: \t\t\t// each instance to potentially have a different runtime type even\n771: \t\t\t// though they all conform to the static schema.\n772: \t\t\tret = cty.ObjectVal(vals)\n773: \t\t} else {\n774: \t\t\tret = cty.EmptyObjectVal\n775: \t\t}\n776: \n777: \tdefault:\n778: \t\tval, ok := instances[addrs.NoKey]\n779: \t\tif !ok {\n780: \t\t\t// if the instance is missing, insert an unknown value\n781: \t\t\tval = cty.UnknownVal(ty)\n782: \t\t}\n783: \n784: \t\tret = val\n785: \t}\n786: \n787: \treturn ret, diags\n788: }\n789: \n790: func (d *evaluationStateData) getEphemeralResource(addr addrs.Resource, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n791: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n792: \n793: \tif d.Operation == walkValidate || d.Operation == walkEval {\n794: \t\t// Ephemeral instances are never live during the validate walk. Eval is\n795: \t\t// similarly offline, and since there is no value stored we can't return\n796: \t\t// anything other than dynamic.\n797: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags\n798: \t}\n799: \n800: \t// Now, we're going to build up a value that represents the resource\n801: \t// or resources that are in the state.\n802: \tinstances := map[addrs.InstanceKey]cty.Value{}\n803: \n804: \t// First, we're going to load any instances that we have written into the\n805: \t// deferrals system. A deferred resource overrides anything that might be\n806: \t// in the state for the resource, so we do this first.\n807: \tfor key, value := range d.Evaluator.Deferrals.GetDeferredResourceInstances(addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)) {\n808: \t\tinstances[key] = value\n809: \t}\n810: \n811: \tabsAddr := addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath)\n812: \tkeyType, keys, haveUnknownKeys := d.Evaluator.Instances.ResourceInstanceKeys(absAddr)\n813: \tif haveUnknownKeys {\n814: \t\t// We can probably do better than totally unknown at least for a\n815: \t\t// single-instance resource, but we'll just keep it simple for now.\n816: \t\t// Result must be marked as ephemeral so that we can still catch\n817: \t\t// attempts to use the results in non-ephemeral locations, so that\n818: \t\t// the operator doesn't end up trapped with an error on a subsequent\n819: \t\t// plan/apply round.\n820: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral), diags\n821: \t}\n822: \n823: \tephems := d.Evaluator.EphemeralResources\n824: \tgetInstValue := func(addr addrs.AbsResourceInstance) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n825: \t\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n826: \n827: \t\t// If we have a deferred instance with this key we don't need to check if it is live or not,\n828: \t\t// it has not been created so we can just return the deferred value.\n829: \t\tif v, ok := instances[addr.Resource.Key]; ok {\n830: \t\t\treturn v, diags\n831: \t\t}\n832: \n833: \t\tval, isLive := ephems.InstanceValue(addr)\n834: \t\tif !isLive {\n835: \t\t\t// If the instance is no longer \"live\" by the time we're accessing\n836: \t\t\t// it then that suggests that it needed renewal and renewal has\n837: \t\t\t// failed, and so the object's value is no longer usable. We'll\n838: \t\t\t// still return the value in case it's somehow useful for diagnosis,\n839: \t\t\t// but we return an error to prevent further evaluation of whatever\n840: \t\t\t// other expression depended on the liveness of this object.\n841: \t\t\t//\n842: \t\t\t// This error message is written on the assumption that it will\n843: \t\t\t// always appear alongside the provider's renewal error, but that'll\n844: \t\t\t// be exposed only once the (now-zombied) ephemeral resource is\n845: \t\t\t// eventually closed, so that we can avoid returning the same error\n846: \t\t\t// multiple times.\n847: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\n848: \t\t\t\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\n849: \t\t\t\tSummary:  \"Ephemeral resource instance has expired\",\n850: \t\t\t\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\n851: \t\t\t\t\t\"The remote object for %s is no longer available due to a renewal error, so Terraform cannot evaluate this expression.\",\n852: \t\t\t\t\taddr,\n853: \t\t\t\t),\n854: \t\t\t\tSubject: rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n855: \t\t\t})\n856: \t\t}\n857: \t\tif val == cty.NilVal {\n858: \t\t\tval = cty.DynamicVal.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\n859: \t\t}\n860: \t\treturn val, diags\n861: \t}\n862: \n863: \tswitch keyType {\n864: \tcase addrs.NoKeyType:\n865: \t\t// For \"no key\" we're returning just a single object representing\n866: \t\t// the single instance of this resource.\n867: \t\tinstVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(absAddr.Instance(addrs.NoKey))\n868: \t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n869: \t\treturn instVal, diags\n870: \tcase addrs.IntKeyType:\n871: \t\t// For integer keys we're returning a tuple-typed value whose\n872: \t\t// indices are the keys.\n873: \t\telems := make([]cty.Value, len(keys))\n874: \t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n875: \t\t\tidx := int(key.(addrs.IntKey))\n876: \t\t\tinstAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)\n877: \t\t\tinstVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)\n878: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n879: \t\t\telems[idx] = instVal\n880: \t\t}\n881: \t\treturn cty.TupleVal(elems), diags\n882: \tcase addrs.StringKeyType:\n883: \t\t// For string keys we're returning an object-typed value whose\n884: \t\t// attributes are the keys.\n885: \t\tattrs := make(map[string]cty.Value, len(keys))\n886: \t\tfor _, key := range keys {\n887: \t\t\tattrName := string(key.(addrs.StringKey))\n888: \t\t\tinstAddr := absAddr.Instance(key)\n889: \t\t\tinstVal, moreDiags := getInstValue(instAddr)\n890: \t\t\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\n891: \t\t\tattrs[attrName] = instVal\n892: \t\t}\n893: \t\treturn cty.ObjectVal(attrs), diags\n894: \tdefault:\n895: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"unhandled instance key type %#v\", keyType))\n896: \t}\n897: }\n898: \n899: func (d *evaluationStateData) getResourceSchema(addr addrs.Resource, providerAddr addrs.Provider) *configschema.Block {\n900: \tschema, _, err := d.Evaluator.Plugins.ResourceTypeSchema(providerAddr, addr.Mode, addr.Type)\n901: \tif err != nil {\n902: \t\t// We have plently other codepaths that will detect and report\n903: \t\t// schema lookup errors before we'd reach this point, so we'll just\n904: \t\t// treat a failure here the same as having no schema.\n905: \t\treturn nil\n906: \t}\n907: \treturn schema\n908: }\n909: \n910: func (d *evaluationStateData) GetOutput(addr addrs.OutputValue, rng tfdiags.SourceRange) (cty.Value, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\n911: \tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\n912: \n913: \t// First we'll make sure the requested value is declared in configuration,\n914: \t// so we can produce a nice message if not.\n915: \tmoduleConfig := d.Evaluator.Config.DescendantForInstance(d.ModulePath)\n916: \tif moduleConfig == nil {\n917: \t\t// should never happen, since we can't be evaluating in a module\n918: \t\t// that wasn't mentioned in configuration.\n919: \t\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\"output value read from %s, which has no configuration\", d.ModulePath))\n920: \t}\n921: \n922: \tconfig := \t\t\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`An output value with the name %q has not been declared.%s`, addr.Name, suggestion),\n937: \t\t\tSubject:  rng.ToHCL().Ptr(),\n938: \t\t})\n939: \t\treturn cty.DynamicVal, up and\n945: \t\t// pass it on.\n946: \t\toutput = &states.OutputValue{\n947: \t\t\tAddr:      addr.Absolute(d.ModulePath),\n948: \t\t\tValue:     cty.NilVal,\n949: \t\t\tSensitive: config.Sensitive,\n950: \t\t}\n951: \t} else if output.Value == cty.NilVal || output.Value.IsNull() {\n952: \t\t// Then we did get a value but Terraform itself thought it was NilVal\n953: \t\t// so we val.Mark(marks.Sensitive)\n960: \t}\n961: \n962: \treturn val, diags\n963: }\n964: \n965: // moduleDisplayAddr returns a string describing the given module instance\n966: // address that is appropriate for returning to users in situations where the\n967: // root module is possible. Specifically, it returns \"the root module\" if the\n968: // root module instance is given, or a string representation of the module\n969: // address otherwise.\n970: func moduleDisplayAddr(addr addrs.ModuleInstance) string {\n971: \tswitch {\n972: \tcase addr.IsRoot():\n973: \t\treturn \"the root module\"\n974: \tdefault:\n975: \t\treturn addr.String()\n976: \t}\n977: }\n978: ","url":"https://sourcegraph.sourcegraph.com/github.com/sg-evals/terraform--v1.10.3@9802216fb24076bc614d9fa7961a9d85f6d7ec48/-/blob/internal/terraform/evaluate.go"}}}

In such a case, the failing secret value should not be\\n128: \\t\\t\\t// displayed to the operator\\n129: \\t\\t\\tif cfg.Sensitive {\\n130: \\t\\t\\t\\tdetail = fmt.Sprintf(\\n131: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The given value is not suitable for %s, which is sensitive: %s. Invalid value defined at %s.\\\",\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr, err, sourceRange.ToHCL(),\\n133: \\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\tsubject = cfg.DeclRange.Ptr()\\n135: \\t\\t\\t}\\n136: \\t\\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n139: \\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n140: \\t\\t\\tSummary:  \\\"Invalid value for input variable\\\",\\n141: \\t\\t\\tDetail:   detail,\\n142: \\t\\t\\tSubject:  subject,\\n143: \\t\\t})\\n144: \\t\\t// We'll return a placeholder unknown value to avoid producing\\n145: \\t\\t// redundant downstream errors.\\n146: \\t\\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cfg.Type), diags\\n147: \\t}\\n148: \\n149: \\t// By the time we get here, we know:\\n150: \\t// - val matches the variable's type constraint\\n151: \\t// - val is definitely not cty.NilVal, but might be a null value if the given was already null.\\n152: \\t//\\n153: \\t// That means we just need to handle the case where the value is null,\\n154: \\t// which might mean we need to use the default value, or produce an error.\\n155: \\t//\\n156: \\t// For historical reasons we do this only for a \\\"non-nullable\\\" variable.\\n157: \\t// Nullable variables just appear as null if they were set to null,\\n158: \\t// regardless of any default value.\\n159: \\tif val.IsNull() && !cfg.Nullable {\\n160: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] prepareFinalInputVariableValue: %s is defined as null\\\", addr)\\n161: \\t\\tif defaultVal != cty.NilVal {\\n162: \\t\\t\\tval = defaultVal\\n163: \\t\\t} else {\\n164: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[ERROR] prepareFinalInputVariableValue: %s is non-nullable but set to null, and is required\\\", addr)\\n165: \\t\\t\\tif nonFileSource != \\\"\\\" {\\n166: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n167: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n168: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  `Required variable not set`,\\n169: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\\n170: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Unsuitable value for %s %s: required variable may not be set to null.\\\",\\n171: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr, nonFileSource,\\n172: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n173: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject: cfg.DeclRange.Ptr(),\\n174: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n175: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n176: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n177: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n178: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  `Required variable not set`,\\n179: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\\n180: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The given value is not suitable for %s defined at %s: required variable may not be set to null.\\\",\\n181: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr, cfg.DeclRange.String(),\\n182: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n183: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject: sourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr(),\\n184: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n185: \\t\\t\\t}\\n186: \\t\\t\\t// Stub out our return value so that the semantic checker doesn't\\n187: \\t\\t\\t// produce redundant downstream errors.\\n188: \\t\\t\\tval = cty.UnknownVal(cfg.Type)\\n189: \\t\\t}\\n190: \\t}\\n191: \\n192: \\tif cfg.Ephemeral {\\n193: \\t\\t// An ephemeral input variable always has an ephemeral value inside the\\n194: \\t\\t// module, even if the value assigned to it from outside is not. This\\n195: \\t\\t// is a useful simplification so that module authors can be explicit\\n196: \\t\\t// about what guarantees they are intending to make (regardless of\\n197: \\t\\t// current implementation details). Changing the ephemerality of an\\n198: \\t\\t// input variable is a breaking change to a module's API.\\n199: \\t\\tval = val.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\\n200: \\t} else {\\n201: \\t\\tif marks.Contains(val, marks.Ephemeral) {\\n202: \\t\\t\\tvar subject hcl.Range\\n203: \\t\\t\\tif raw.HasSourceRange() {\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\tsubject = raw.SourceRange.ToHCL()\\n205: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\t// We shouldn't typically get here for ephemeral values, because\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\t// all of the source types that can represent expressions that\\n208: \\t\\t\\t\\t// could potentially produce ephemeral values are those which\\n209: \\t\\t\\t\\t// have source locations. This is just here for robustness.\\n210: \\t\\t\\t\\tsubject = cfg.DeclRange\\n211: \\t\\t\\t}\\n212: \\n213: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n214: \\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n215: \\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  \\\"Ephemeral value not allowed\\\",\\n216: \\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:   \\\"This input variable is not declared as accepting a ephemeral values, so it cannot be set to a result derived from an ephemeral value.\\\",\\n217: \\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:  subject.Ptr(),\\n218: \\t\\t\\t})\\n219: \\t\\t}\\n220: \\t}\\n221: \\n222: \\treturn val, diags\\n223: }\\n224: \\n225: // evalVariableValidations ensures that all of the configured custom validations\\n226: // for a variable are passing.\\n227: //\\n228: // This must be used only after any side-effects that make the value of the\\n229: // variable available for use in expression evaluation, such as\\n230: // EvalModuleCallArgument for variables in descendant modules.\\n231: func evalVariableValidations(addr addrs.AbsInputVariableInstance, ctx EvalContext, rules []*configs.CheckRule, valueRng hcl.Range, validateWalk bool) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n232: \\tif len(rules) == 0 {\\n233: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: no validation rules declared for %s, so skipping\\\", addr)\\n234: \\t\\treturn nil\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: validating %s\\\", addr)\\n237: \\n238: \\tcheckState := ctx.Checks()\\n239: \\tif !checkState.ConfigHasChecks(addr.ConfigCheckable()) {\\n240: \\t\\t// We have nothing to do if this object doesn't have any checks,\\n241: \\t\\t// but the \\\"rules\\\" slice should agree that we don't.\\n242: \\t\\tif ct := len(rules); ct != 0 {\\n243: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"check state says that %s should have no rules, but it has %d\\\", addr, ct))\\n244: \\t\\t}\\n245: \\t\\treturn diags\\n246: \\t}\\n247: \\n248: \\t// We'll build just one evaluation context covering the data needed by\\n249: \\t// all of the rules together, since that'll minimize lock contention\\n250: \\t// on the state, plan, etc.\\n251: \\tscope := ctx.EvaluationScope(nil, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)\\n252: \\tvar refs []*addrs.Reference\\n253: \\tfor _, rule := range rules {\\n254: \\t\\tcondRefs, moreDiags := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, rule.Condition)\\n255: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n256: \\t\\tmsgRefs, moreDiags := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, rule.ErrorMessage)\\n257: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n258: \\t\\trefs = append(refs, condRefs...)\\n259: \\t\\trefs = append(refs, msgRefs...)\\n260: \\t}\\n261: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n262: \\t\\t// If any of the references were invalid then evaluating the expressions\\n263: \\t\\t// will duplicate those errors, so we'll bail out early.\\n264: \\t\\treturn diags\\n265: \\t}\\n266: \\thclCtx, moreDiags := scope.EvalContext(refs)\\n267: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n268: \\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\\n269: \\t\\treturn diags\\n270: \\t}\\n271: \\n272: \\t// HACK: Historically we manually built a very constrained hcl.EvalContext\\n273: \\t// here, which only included the value of the one specific input variable\\n274: \\t// we're validating, since we didn't yet support referring to anything\\n275: \\t// else. That accidentally bypassed our rule that input variables are\\n276: \\t// always unknown during the validate walk, and thus accidentally created\\n277: \\t// a useful behavior of actually checking constant-only values against\\n278: \\t// their validation rules just during \\\"terraform validate\\\", rather than\\n279: \\t// having to run \\\"terraform plan\\\".\\n280: \\t//\\n281: \\t// Although that behavior was accidental, it makes simple validation rules\\n282: \\t// more useful and is protected by compatibility promises, and so we'll\\n283: \\t// fake it here by overwriting the unknown value that scope.EvalContext\\n284: \\t// will have inserted with a possibly-more-known value using the same\\n285: \\t// strategy our special code used to use.\\n286: \\tourVal := ctx.NamedValues().GetInputVariableValue(addr)\\n287: \\tif ourVal != cty.NilVal {\\n288: \\t\\t// (it would be weird for ourVal to be nil here, but we'll tolerate it\\n289: \\t\\t// because it was scope.EvalContext's responsibility to check for the\\n290: \\t\\t// absent final value, and even if it didn't we'll just get an\\n291: \\t\\t// evaluation error when evaluating the expressions below anyway.)\\n292: \\n293: \\t\\t// Our goal here is to make sure that a reference to the variable\\n294: \\t\\t// we're checking will evaluate to ourVal, regardless of what else\\n295: \\t\\t// scope.EvalContext might have put in the variables table.\\n296: \\t\\tif hclCtx.Variables == nil {\\n297: \\t\\t\\thclCtx.Variables = make(map[string]cty.Value)\\n298: \\t\\t}\\n299: \\t\\tif varsVal, ok := hclCtx.Variables[\\\"var\\\"]; ok {\\n300: \\t\\t\\t// Unfortunately we need to unpack and repack the object here,\\n301: \\t\\t\\t// because cty values are immutable.\\n302: \\t\\t\\tattrs := varsVal.AsValueMap()\\n303: \\t\\t\\tattrs[addr.Variable.Name] = ourVal\\n304: \\t\\t\\thclCtx.Variables[\\\"var\\\"] = cty.ObjectVal(attrs)\\n305: \\t\\t} else {\\n306: \\t\\t\\thclCtx.Variables[\\\"var\\\"] = cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\\n307: \\t\\t\\t\\taddr.Variable.Name: ourVal,\\n308: \\t\\t\\t})\\n309: \\t\\t}\\n310: \\t}\\n311: \\n312: \\tfor ix, validation := range rules {\\n313: \\t\\tresult, ruleDiags := evalVariableValidation(validation, hclCtx, valueRng, addr, ix, validateWalk)\\n314: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ruleDiags)\\n315: \\n316: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: %s status is now %s\\\", addr, result.Status)\\n317: \\t\\tif result.Status == checks.StatusFail {\\n318: \\t\\t\\tcheckState.ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.InputValidation, ix, result.FailureMessage)\\n319: \\t\\t} else {\\n320: \\t\\t\\tcheckState.ReportCheckResult(addr, addrs.InputValidation, ix, result.Status)\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t}\\n323: \\n324: \\treturn diags\\n325: }\\n326: \\n327: func evalVariableValidation(validation *configs.CheckRule, hclCtx *hcl.EvalContext, valueRng hcl.Range, addr addrs.AbsInputVariableInstance, ix int, validateWalk bool) (checkResult, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n328: \\tconst errInvalidCondition = \\\"Invalid variable validation result\\\"\\n329: \\tconst errInvalidValue = \\\"Invalid value for variable\\\"\\n330: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n331: \\n332: \\tresult, moreDiags := validation.Condition.Value(hclCtx)\\n333: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n334: \\terrorValue, errorDiags := validation.ErrorMessage.Value(hclCtx)\\n335: \\n336: \\t// The following error handling is a workaround to preserve backwards\\n337: \\t// compatibility. Due to an implementation quirk, all prior versions of\\n338: \\t// Terraform would treat error messages specified using JSON\\n339: \\t// configuration syntax (.tf.json) as string literals, even if they\\n340: \\t// contained the \\\"${\\\" template expression operator. This behaviour did\\n341: \\t// not match that of HCL configuration syntax, where a template\\n342: \\t// expression would result in a validation error.\\n343: \\t//\\n344: \\t// As a result, users writing or generating JSON configuration syntax\\n345: \\t// may have specified error messages which are invalid template\\n346: \\t// expressions. As we add support for error message expressions, we are\\n347: \\t// unable to perfectly distinguish between these two cases.\\n348: \\t//\\n349: \\t// To ensure that we don't break backwards compatibility, we have the\\n350: \\t// below fallback logic if the error message fails to evaluate. This\\n351: \\t// should only have any effect for JSON configurations. The gohcl\\n352: \\t// DecodeExpression function behaves differently when the source of the\\n353: \\t// expression is a JSON configuration file and a nil context is passed.\\n354: \\tif errorDiags.HasErrors() {\\n355: \\t\\t// Attempt to decode the expression as a string literal. Passing\\n356: \\t\\t// nil as the context forces a JSON syntax string value to be\\n357: \\t\\t// interpreted as a string literal.\\n358: \\t\\tvar errorString string\\n359: \\t\\tmoreErrorDiags := gohcl.DecodeExpression(validation.ErrorMessage, nil, &errorString)\\n360: \\t\\tif !moreErrorDiags.HasErrors() {\\n361: \\t\\t\\t// Decoding succeeded, meaning that this is a JSON syntax\\n362: \\t\\t\\t// string value. We rewrap that as a cty value to allow later\\n363: \\t\\t\\t// decoding to succeed.\\n364: \\t\\t\\terrorValue = cty.StringVal(errorString)\\n365: \\n366: \\t\\t\\t// This warning diagnostic explains this odd behaviour, while\\n367: \\t\\t\\t// giving us an escape hatch to change this to a hard failure\\n368: \\t\\t\\t// in some future Terraform 1.x version.\\n369: \\t\\t\\terrorDiags = hcl.Diagnostics{\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\t&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n371: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagWarning,\\n372: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:     \\\"Validation error message expression is invalid\\\",\\n373: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:      fmt.Sprintf(\\\"The error message provided could not be evaluated as an expression, so Terraform is interpreting it as a string literal.\\\\n\\\\nIn future versions of Terraform, this will be considered an error. Please file a GitHub issue if this would break your workflow.\\\\n\\\\n%s\\\", errorDiags.Error()),\\n374: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.ErrorMessage.Range().Ptr(),\\n375: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tContext:     validation.DeclRange.Ptr(),\\n376: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.ErrorMessage,\\n377: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n379: \\t\\t\\t}\\n380: \\t\\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\t\\t// We want to either report the original diagnostics if the\\n383: \\t\\t// fallback failed, or the warning generated above if it succeeded.\\n384: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(errorDiags)\\n385: \\t}\\n386: \\n387: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n388: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: %s rule %s check rule evaluation failed: %s\\\", addr, validation.DeclRange, diags.Err().Error())\\n389: \\t}\\n390: \\tif !result.IsKnown() {\\n391: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: %s rule %s condition value is unknown, so skipping validation for now\\\", addr, validation.DeclRange)\\n392: \\n393: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusUnknown}, diags // We'll wait until we've learned more, then.\\n394: \\t}\\n395: \\tif result.IsNull() {\\n396: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n397: \\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n398: \\t\\t\\tSummary:     errInvalidCondition,\\n399: \\t\\t\\tDetail:      \\\"Validation condition expression must return either true or false, not null.\\\",\\n400: \\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.Condition.Range().Ptr(),\\n401: \\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.Condition,\\n402: \\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n403: \\t\\t})\\n404: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusError}, diags\\n405: \\t}\\n406: \\tvar err error\\n407: \\tresult, err = convert.Convert(result, cty.Bool)\\n408: \\tif err != nil {\\n409: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n410: \\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n411: \\t\\t\\tSummary:     errInvalidCondition,\\n412: \\t\\t\\tDetail:      fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Invalid validation condition result value: %s.\\\", tfdiags.FormatError(err)),\\n413: \\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.Condition.Range().Ptr(),\\n414: \\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.Condition,\\n415: \\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n416: \\t\\t})\\n417: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusError}, diags\\n418: \\t}\\n419: \\n420: \\t// Validation condition may be marked if the input variable is bound to\\n421: \\t// a sensitive value. This is irrelevant to the validation process, so\\n422: \\t// we discard the marks now.\\n423: \\tresult, _ = result.Unmark()\\n424: \\tstatus := checks.StatusForCtyValue(result)\\n425: \\n426: \\tif status != checks.StatusFail {\\n427: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: status}, diags\\n428: \\t}\\n429: \\n430: \\tif !errorValue.IsKnown() {\\n431: \\t\\tif validateWalk {\\n432: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] evalVariableValidations: %s rule %s error_message value is unknown, so skipping validation for now\\\", addr, validation.DeclRange)\\n433: \\t\\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusUnknown}, diags\\n434: \\t\\t}\\n435: \\n436: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n437: \\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n438: \\t\\t\\tSummary:     \\\"Invalid error message\\\",\\n439: \\t\\t\\tDetail:      \\\"Unsuitable value for error message: expression refers to values that won't be known until the apply phase.\\\",\\n440: \\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.ErrorMessage.Range().Ptr(),\\n441: \\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.ErrorMessage,\\n442: \\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n443: \\t\\t\\tExtra:       diagnosticCausedByUnknown(true),\\n444: \\t\\t})\\n445: \\t\\treturn checkResult{\\n446: \\t\\t\\tStatus: checks.StatusError,\\n447: \\t\\t}, diags\\n448: \\t}\\n449: \\n450: \\tvar errorMessage Terraform will not display the resulting message.\\n470: \\n471: You can correct this by removing references to sensitive values, or by carefully using the nonsensitive() function if the expression will not reveal the sensitive data.`,\\n472: \\n473: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.ErrorMessage.Range().Ptr(),\\n474: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.ErrorMessage,\\n475: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n476: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n477: \\t\\t\\t\\terrorMessage = \\\"The error message included a sensitive value, so it will not be displayed.\\\"\\n478: \\t\\t\\t} else if marks.Has(errorValue, marks.Ephemeral) {\\n479: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n480: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n481: \\n482: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary: \\\"Error message refers to ephemeral values\\\",\\n483: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: `The error expression used to explain this condition refers to ephemeral values. \\\"Invalid default value for module argument\\\",\\n41: \\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\\n42: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The default value for variable %q is incompatible with its type constraint: %s.\\\",\\n43: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tcfg.Name, err,\\n44: \\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n45: \\t\\t\\t\\tSubject: &cfg.DeclRange,\\n46: \\t\\t\\t})\\n47: \\t\\t\\t// We'll return a placeholder unknown value to avoid producing\\n48: \\t\\t\\t// redundant downstream errors.\\n49: \\t\\t\\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cfg.Type), diags\\n50: \\t\\t}\\n51: \\t}\\n52: \\n53: \\tvar sourceRange tfdiags.SourceRange\\n54: \\tvar nonFileSource string\\n55: \\tif raw.HasSourceRange() {\\n56: \\t\\tsourceRange = raw.SourceRange\\n57: \\t} else {\\n58: \\t\\t// If the value came from a place that isn't a file and thus doesn't\\n59: \\t\\t// have its own source range, we'll use the declaration range as\\n60: \\t\\t// our source range and generate some slightly different error\\n61: \\t\\t// messages.\\n62: \\t\\tsourceRange = tfdiags.SourceRangeFromHCL(cfg.DeclRange)\\n63: \\t\\tswitch raw.SourceType {\\n64: \\t\\tcase ValueFromCLIArg:\\n65: \\t\\t\\tnonFileSource = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"set using -var=\\\\\\\"%s=...\\\\\\\"\\\", addr.Variable.Name)\\n66: \\t\\tcase ValueFromEnvVar:\\n67: \\t\\t\\tnonFileSource = fmt.Sprintf(\\\"set using the TF_VAR_%s environment variable\\\", addr.Variable.Name)\\n68: \\t\\tcase ValueFromInput:\\n69: \\t\\t\\tnonFileSource = \\\"set using an interactive prompt\\\"\\n70: \\t\\tdefault:\\n71: \\t\\t\\tnonFileSource = \\\"set from outside of the configuration\\\"\\n72: \\t\\t}\\n73: \\t}\\n74: \\n75: \\tgiven := raw.Value\\n76: \\tif given == cty.NilVal { // The variable wasn't set at all (even to null)\\n77: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] prepareFinalInputVariableValue: %s has no defined value\\\", addr)\\n78: \\t\\tif cfg.Required() {\\n79: \\t\\t\\t// NOTE: The CLI layer typically checks for itself whether all of\\n80: \\t\\t\\t// the required _root_ module variables are set, which would\\n81: \\t\\t\\t// mask this error with a more specific one that refers to the\\n82: \\t\\t\\t// CLI features for setting such variables. We can get here for\\n83: \\t\\t\\t// child module variables, though.\\n84: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[ERROR] prepareFinalInputVariableValue: %s is required but is not set\\\", addr)\\n85: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n86: \\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n87: \\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  `Required variable not set`,\\n88: \\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:   fmt.Sprintf(`The variable %q is required, but is not set.`, addr.Variable.Name),\\n89: \\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:  cfg.DeclRange.Ptr(),\\n90: \\t\\t\\t})\\n91: \\t\\t\\t// We'll return a placeholder unknown value to avoid producing\\n92: \\t\\t\\t// redundant downstream errors.\\n93: \\t\\t\\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cfg.Type), diags\\n94: \\t\\t}\\n95: \\n96: \\t\\tgiven = defaultVal // must be set, because we checked above that the variable isn't required\\n97: \\t}\\n98: \\n99: \\t// Apply defaults from the variable's type constraint to the converted value,\\n100: \\t// unless the converted value is null. We do not apply defaults to top-level\\n101: \\t// null values, as doing so could prevent assigning null to a nullable\\n102: \\t// variable.\\n103: \\tif cfg.TypeDefaults != nil && !given.IsNull() {\\n104: \\t\\tgiven = cfg.TypeDefaults.Apply(given)\\n105: \\t}\\n106: \\n107: \\tval, err := convert.Convert(given, convertTy)\\n108: \\tif err != nil {\\n109: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[ERROR] prepareFinalInputVariableValue: %s has unsuitable type\\\\n  got:  %s\\\\n  want: %s\\\", addr, given.Type(), convertTy)\\n110: \\t\\tvar detail string\\n111: \\t\\tvar subject *hcl.Range\\n112: \\t\\tif nonFileSource != \\\"\\\" {\\n113: \\t\\t\\tdetail = fmt.Sprintf(\\n114: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Unsuitable value for %s %s: %s.\\\",\\n115: \\t\\t\\t\\taddr, nonFileSource, err,\\n116: \\t\\t\\t)\\n117: \\t\\t\\tsubject = cfg.DeclRange.Ptr()\\n118: \\t\\t} else {\\n119: \\t\\t\\tdetail = fmt.Sprintf(\\n120: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The given value is not suitable for %s declared at %s: %s.\\\",\\n121: \\t\\t\\t\\taddr, cfg.DeclRange.String(), err,\\n122: \\t\\t\\t)\\n123: \\t\\t\\tsubject = sourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr()\\n124: \\n125: \\t\\t\\t// In some workflows, the operator running terraform does not have access to the variables\\n126: \\t\\t\\t// themselves. They are for example stored in encrypted files that will be used by the CI toolset\\n127: \\t\\t\\t// and not by the operator directly. In such a case, the failing secret value should not be\\n128: \\t\\t\\t// displayed to the operator\\n129: \\t\\t\\tif cfg.Sensitive {\\n130: \\t\\t\\t\\tdetail = fmt.Sprintf(\\n131: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The given value is not suitable for %s, which is sensitive: %s. Invalid value defined at %s.\\\",\\n132: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr, err, sourceRange.ToHCL(),\\n133: \\t\\t\\t\\t)\\n134: \\t\\t\\t\\tsubject = cfg.DeclRange.Ptr()\\n135: \\t\\t\\t}\\n136: \\t\\t}\\n137: \\n138: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n139: \\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n140: \\t\\t\\tSummary:  \\\"Invalid value for input variable\\\",\\n141: \\t\\t\\tDetail:   detail,\\n142: \\t\\t\\tSubject:  subject,\\n143: \\t\\t})\\n144: \\t\\t// We'll return a placeholder unknown value to avoid producing\\n145: \\t\\t// redundant downstream errors.\\n146: \\t\\treturn cty.UnknownVal(cfg.Type), diags\\n147: \\t}\\n148: \\n149: \\t// By the time we get here, we know:\\n150: \\t// - val matches the variable's type constraint\\n151: \\t// - val is definitely not cty.NilVal, but might be a null value if the given was already null.\\n152: \\t//\\n153: \\t// That means we just need to handle the case where the value is null,\\n154: \\t// which might mean we need to use the default value, or produce an error.\\n155: \\t//\\n156: \\t// For historical reasons we do this only for a \\\"non-nullable\\\" variable.\\n157: \\t// Nullable variables just appear as null if they were set to null,\\n158: \\t// regardless of any default value.\\n159: \\tif val.IsNull() && !cfg.Nullable {\\n160: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] prepareFinalInputVariableValue: %s is defined as null\\\", addr)\\n161: \\t\\tif defaultVal != cty.NilVal {\\n162: \\t\\t\\tval = defaultVal\\n163: \\t\\t} else {\\n164: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[ERROR] prepareFinalInputVariableValue: %s is non-nullable but set to null, and is required\\\", addr)\\n165: \\t\\t\\tif nonFileSource != \\\"\\\" {\\n166: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n167: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n168: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  `Required variable not set`,\\n169: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\\n170: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"Unsuitable value for %s %s: required variable may not be set to null.\\\",\\n171: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr, nonFileSource,\\n172: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n173: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject: cfg.DeclRange.Ptr(),\\n174: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n175: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n176: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n177: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n178: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  `Required variable not set`,\\n179: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: fmt.Sprintf(\\n180: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\\"The given value is not suitable for %s defined at %s: required variable may not be set to null.\\\",\\n181: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t\\taddr, cfg.DeclRange.String(),\\n182: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\t),\\n183: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject: sourceRange.ToHCL().Ptr(),\\n184: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n185: \\t\\t\\t}\\n186: \\t\\t\\t// Stub out our return value so that the semantic checker doesn't\\n187: \\t\\t\\t// produce redundant downstream errors.\\n188: \\t\\t\\tval = cty.UnknownVal(cfg.Type)\\n189: \\t\\t}\\n190: \\t}\\n191: \\n192: \\tif cfg.Ephemeral {\\n193: \\t\\t// An ephemeral input variable always has an ephemeral value inside the\\n194: \\t\\t// module, even if the value assigned to it from outside is not. This\\n195: \\t\\t// is a useful simplification so that module authors can be explicit\\n196: \\t\\t// about what guarantees they are intending to make (regardless of\\n197: \\t\\t// current implementation details). Changing the ephemerality of an\\n198: \\t\\t// input variable is a breaking change to a module's API.\\n199: \\t\\tval = val.Mark(marks.Ephemeral)\\n200: \\t} else {\\n201: \\t\\tif marks.Contains(val, marks.Ephemeral) {\\n202: \\t\\t\\tvar subject hcl.Range\\n203: \\t\\t\\tif raw.HasSourceRange() {\\n204: \\t\\t\\t\\tsubject = raw.SourceRange.ToHCL()\\n205: \\t\\t\\t} else {\\n206: \\t\\t\\t\\t// We shouldn't typically get here for ephemeral values, because\\n207: \\t\\t\\t\\t// all of the source types that can represent expressions that\\n208: \\t\\t\\t\\t// could potentially produce ephemeral values are those which\\n209: \\t\\t\\t\\t// have source locations. This is just here for robustness.\\n210: \\t\\t\\t\\tsubject = cfg.DeclRange\\n211: \\t\\t\\t}\\n212: \\n213: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n214: \\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n215: \\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:  \\\"Ephemeral value not allowed\\\",\\n216: \\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:   \\\"This input variable is not declared as accepting a ephemeral values, so it cannot be set to a result derived from an ephemeral value.\\\",\\n217: \\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:  subject.Ptr(),\\n218: \\t\\t\\t})\\n219: \\t\\t}\\n220: \\t}\\n221: \\n222: \\treturn val, diags\\n223: }\\n224: \\n225: // evalVariableValidations ensures that all of the configured custom validations\\n226: // for a variable are passing.\\n227: //\\n228: // This must be used only after any side-effects that make the value of the\\n229: // variable available for use in expression evaluation, such as\\n230: // EvalModuleCallArgument for variables in descendant modules.\\n231: func evalVariableValidations(addr addrs.AbsInputVariableInstance, ctx EvalContext, rules []*configs.CheckRule, valueRng hcl.Range, validateWalk bool) (diags tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n232: \\tif len(rules) == 0 {\\n233: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: no validation rules declared for %s, so skipping\\\", addr)\\n234: \\t\\treturn nil\\n235: \\t}\\n236: \\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: validating %s\\\", addr)\\n237: \\n238: \\tcheckState := ctx.Checks()\\n239: \\tif !checkState.ConfigHasChecks(addr.ConfigCheckable()) {\\n240: \\t\\t// We have nothing to do if this object doesn't have any checks,\\n241: \\t\\t// but the \\\"rules\\\" slice should agree that we don't.\\n242: \\t\\tif ct := len(rules); ct != 0 {\\n243: \\t\\t\\tpanic(fmt.Sprintf(\\\"check state says that %s should have no rules, but it has %d\\\", addr, ct))\\n244: \\t\\t}\\n245: \\t\\treturn diags\\n246: \\t}\\n247: \\n248: \\t// We'll build just one evaluation context covering the data needed by\\n249: \\t// all of the rules together, since that'll minimize lock contention\\n250: \\t// on the state, plan, etc.\\n251: \\tscope := ctx.EvaluationScope(nil, nil, EvalDataForNoInstanceKey)\\n252: \\tvar refs []*addrs.Reference\\n253: \\tfor _, rule := range rules {\\n254: \\t\\tcondRefs, moreDiags := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, rule.Condition)\\n255: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n256: \\t\\tmsgRefs, moreDiags := langrefs.ReferencesInExpr(addrs.ParseRef, rule.ErrorMessage)\\n257: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n258: \\t\\trefs = append(refs, condRefs...)\\n259: \\t\\trefs = append(refs, msgRefs...)\\n260: \\t}\\n261: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n262: \\t\\t// If any of the references were invalid then evaluating the expressions\\n263: \\t\\t// will duplicate those errors, so we'll bail out early.\\n264: \\t\\treturn diags\\n265: \\t}\\n266: \\thclCtx, moreDiags := scope.EvalContext(refs)\\n267: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n268: \\tif moreDiags.HasErrors() {\\n269: \\t\\treturn diags\\n270: \\t}\\n271: \\n272: \\t// HACK: Historically we manually built a very constrained hcl.EvalContext\\n273: \\t// here, which only included the value of the one specific input variable\\n274: \\t// we're validating, since we didn't yet support referring to anything\\n275: \\t// else. That accidentally bypassed our rule that input variables are\\n276: \\t// always unknown during the validate walk, and thus accidentally created\\n277: \\t// a useful behavior of actually checking constant-only values against\\n278: \\t// their validation rules just during \\\"terraform validate\\\", rather than\\n279: \\t// having to run \\\"terraform plan\\\".\\n280: \\t//\\n281: \\t// Although that behavior was accidental, it makes simple validation rules\\n282: \\t// more useful and is protected by compatibility promises, and so we'll\\n283: \\t// fake it here by overwriting the unknown value that scope.EvalContext\\n284: \\t// will have inserted with a possibly-more-known value using the same\\n285: \\t// strategy our special code used to use.\\n286: \\tourVal := ctx.NamedValues().GetInputVariableValue(addr)\\n287: \\tif ourVal != cty.NilVal {\\n288: \\t\\t// (it would be weird for ourVal to be nil here, but we'll tolerate it\\n289: \\t\\t// because it was scope.EvalContext's responsibility to check for the\\n290: \\t\\t// absent final value, and even if it didn't we'll just get an\\n291: \\t\\t// evaluation error when evaluating the expressions below anyway.)\\n292: \\n293: \\t\\t// Our goal here is to make sure that a reference to the variable\\n294: \\t\\t// we're checking will evaluate to ourVal, regardless of what else\\n295: \\t\\t// scope.EvalContext might have put in the variables table.\\n296: \\t\\tif hclCtx.Variables == nil {\\n297: \\t\\t\\thclCtx.Variables = make(map[string]cty.Value)\\n298: \\t\\t}\\n299: \\t\\tif varsVal, ok := hclCtx.Variables[\\\"var\\\"]; ok {\\n300: \\t\\t\\t// Unfortunately we need to unpack and repack the object here,\\n301: \\t\\t\\t// because cty values are immutable.\\n302: \\t\\t\\tattrs := varsVal.AsValueMap()\\n303: \\t\\t\\tattrs[addr.Variable.Name] = ourVal\\n304: \\t\\t\\thclCtx.Variables[\\\"var\\\"] = cty.ObjectVal(attrs)\\n305: \\t\\t} else {\\n306: \\t\\t\\thclCtx.Variables[\\\"var\\\"] = cty.ObjectVal(map[string]cty.Value{\\n307: \\t\\t\\t\\taddr.Variable.Name: ourVal,\\n308: \\t\\t\\t})\\n309: \\t\\t}\\n310: \\t}\\n311: \\n312: \\tfor ix, validation := range rules {\\n313: \\t\\tresult, ruleDiags := evalVariableValidation(validation, hclCtx, valueRng, addr, ix, validateWalk)\\n314: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(ruleDiags)\\n315: \\n316: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: %s status is now %s\\\", addr, result.Status)\\n317: \\t\\tif result.Status == checks.StatusFail {\\n318: \\t\\t\\tcheckState.ReportCheckFailure(addr, addrs.InputValidation, ix, result.FailureMessage)\\n319: \\t\\t} else {\\n320: \\t\\t\\tcheckState.ReportCheckResult(addr, addrs.InputValidation, ix, result.Status)\\n321: \\t\\t}\\n322: \\t}\\n323: \\n324: \\treturn diags\\n325: }\\n326: \\n327: func evalVariableValidation(validation *configs.CheckRule, hclCtx *hcl.EvalContext, valueRng hcl.Range, addr addrs.AbsInputVariableInstance, ix int, validateWalk bool) (checkResult, tfdiags.Diagnostics) {\\n328: \\tconst errInvalidCondition = \\\"Invalid variable validation result\\\"\\n329: \\tconst errInvalidValue = \\\"Invalid value for variable\\\"\\n330: \\tvar diags tfdiags.Diagnostics\\n331: \\n332: \\tresult, moreDiags := validation.Condition.Value(hclCtx)\\n333: \\tdiags = diags.Append(moreDiags)\\n334: \\terrorValue, errorDiags := validation.ErrorMessage.Value(hclCtx)\\n335: \\n336: \\t// The following error handling is a workaround to preserve backwards\\n337: \\t// compatibility. Due to an implementation quirk, all prior versions of\\n338: \\t// Terraform would treat error messages specified using JSON\\n339: \\t// configuration syntax (.tf.json) as string literals, even if they\\n340: \\t// contained the \\\"${\\\" template expression operator. This behaviour did\\n341: \\t// not match that of HCL configuration syntax, where a template\\n342: \\t// expression would result in a validation error.\\n343: \\t//\\n344: \\t// As a result, users writing or generating JSON configuration syntax\\n345: \\t// may have specified error messages which are invalid template\\n346: \\t// expressions. As we add support for error message expressions, we are\\n347: \\t// unable to perfectly distinguish between these two cases.\\n348: \\t//\\n349: \\t// To ensure that we don't break backwards compatibility, we have the\\n350: \\t// below fallback logic if the error message fails to evaluate. This\\n351: \\t// should only have any effect for JSON configurations. The gohcl\\n352: \\t// DecodeExpression function behaves differently when the source of the\\n353: \\t// expression is a JSON configuration file and a nil context is passed.\\n354: \\tif errorDiags.HasErrors() {\\n355: \\t\\t// Attempt to decode the expression as a string literal. Passing\\n356: \\t\\t// nil as the context forces a JSON syntax string value to be\\n357: \\t\\t// interpreted as a string literal.\\n358: \\t\\tvar errorString string\\n359: \\t\\tmoreErrorDiags := gohcl.DecodeExpression(validation.ErrorMessage, nil, &errorString)\\n360: \\t\\tif !moreErrorDiags.HasErrors() {\\n361: \\t\\t\\t// Decoding succeeded, meaning that this is a JSON syntax\\n362: \\t\\t\\t// string value. We rewrap that as a cty value to allow later\\n363: \\t\\t\\t// decoding to succeed.\\n364: \\t\\t\\terrorValue = cty.StringVal(errorString)\\n365: \\n366: \\t\\t\\t// This warning diagnostic explains this odd behaviour, while\\n367: \\t\\t\\t// giving us an escape hatch to change this to a hard failure\\n368: \\t\\t\\t// in some future Terraform 1.x version.\\n369: \\t\\t\\terrorDiags = hcl.Diagnostics{\\n370: \\t\\t\\t\\t&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n371: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagWarning,\\n372: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:     \\\"Validation error message expression is invalid\\\",\\n373: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail:      fmt.Sprintf(\\\"The error message provided could not be evaluated as an expression, so Terraform is interpreting it as a string literal.\\\\n\\\\nIn future versions of Terraform, this will be considered an error. Please file a GitHub issue if this would break your workflow.\\\\n\\\\n%s\\\", errorDiags.Error()),\\n374: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.ErrorMessage.Range().Ptr(),\\n375: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tContext:     validation.DeclRange.Ptr(),\\n376: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.ErrorMessage,\\n377: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n378: \\t\\t\\t\\t},\\n379: \\t\\t\\t}\\n380: \\t\\t}\\n381: \\n382: \\t\\t// We want to either report the original diagnostics if the\\n383: \\t\\t// fallback failed, or the warning generated above if it succeeded.\\n384: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(errorDiags)\\n385: \\t}\\n386: \\n387: \\tif diags.HasErrors() {\\n388: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: %s rule %s check rule evaluation failed: %s\\\", addr, validation.DeclRange, diags.Err().Error())\\n389: \\t}\\n390: \\tif !result.IsKnown() {\\n391: \\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[TRACE] evalVariableValidations: %s rule %s condition value is unknown, so skipping validation for now\\\", addr, validation.DeclRange)\\n392: \\n393: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusUnknown}, diags // We'll wait until we've learned more, then.\\n394: \\t}\\n395: \\tif result.IsNull() {\\n396: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n397: \\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n398: \\t\\t\\tSummary:     errInvalidCondition,\\n399: \\t\\t\\tDetail:      \\\"Validation condition expression must return either true or false, not null.\\\",\\n400: \\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.Condition.Range().Ptr(),\\n401: \\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.Condition,\\n402: \\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n403: \\t\\t})\\n404: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusError}, diags\\n405: \\t}\\n406: \\tvar err error\\n407: \\tresult, err = convert.Convert(result, cty.Bool)\\n408: \\tif err != nil {\\n409: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n410: \\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n411: \\t\\t\\tSummary:     errInvalidCondition,\\n412: \\t\\t\\tDetail:      fmt.Sprintf(\\\"Invalid validation condition result value: %s.\\\", tfdiags.FormatError(err)),\\n413: \\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.Condition.Range().Ptr(),\\n414: \\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.Condition,\\n415: \\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n416: \\t\\t})\\n417: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusError}, diags\\n418: \\t}\\n419: \\n420: \\t// Validation condition may be marked if the input variable is bound to\\n421: \\t// a sensitive value. This is irrelevant to the validation process, so\\n422: \\t// we discard the marks now.\\n423: \\tresult, _ = result.Unmark()\\n424: \\tstatus := checks.StatusForCtyValue(result)\\n425: \\n426: \\tif status != checks.StatusFail {\\n427: \\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: status}, diags\\n428: \\t}\\n429: \\n430: \\tif !errorValue.IsKnown() {\\n431: \\t\\tif validateWalk {\\n432: \\t\\t\\tlog.Printf(\\\"[DEBUG] evalVariableValidations: %s rule %s error_message value is unknown, so skipping validation for now\\\", addr, validation.DeclRange)\\n433: \\t\\t\\treturn checkResult{Status: checks.StatusUnknown}, diags\\n434: \\t\\t}\\n435: \\n436: \\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n437: \\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n438: \\t\\t\\tSummary:     \\\"Invalid error message\\\",\\n439: \\t\\t\\tDetail:      \\\"Unsuitable value for error message: expression refers to values that won't be known until the apply phase.\\\",\\n440: \\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.ErrorMessage.Range().Ptr(),\\n441: \\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.ErrorMessage,\\n442: \\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n443: \\t\\t\\tExtra: string\\n451: \\tif !errorDiags.HasErrors() && !errorValue.IsNull() {\\n452: \\t\\tvar err error\\n453: \\t\\terrorValue, err = convert.Convert(errorValue, cty.String)\\n454: \\t\\tif err != nil {\\n455: \\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n456: \\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n457: \\t\\t\\t\\tSummary:     \\\"Invalid error message\\\",\\n458: \\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n466: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n467: \\n468: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary: \\\"Error message refers to sensitive values\\\",\\n469: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: `The error expression used to explain this condition refers to sensitive values. Terraform will not display the resulting message.\\n470: \\n471: You can correct this by removing references to sensitive values, or by carefully using the nonsensitive() function if the expression will not reveal the sensitive data.`,\\n472: \\n473: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSubject:     validation.ErrorMessage.Range().Ptr(),\\n474: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tExpression:  validation.ErrorMessage,\\n475: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tEvalContext: hclCtx,\\n476: \\t\\t\\t\\t})\\n477: \\t\\t\\t\\terrorMessage = \\\"The error message included a sensitive value, so it will not be displayed.\\\"\\n478: \\t\\t\\t} else if marks.Has(errorValue, marks.Ephemeral) {\\n479: \\t\\t\\t\\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n480: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSeverity: hcl.DiagError,\\n481: \\n482: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tSummary: \\\"Error message refers to ephemeral values\\\",\\n483: \\t\\t\\t\\t\\tDetail: `The error expression used to explain this condition refers to ephemeral values. to evaluate condition error message.\\\"\\n499: \\t}\\n500: \\n501: \\tdiags = diags.Append(&hcl.Diagnostic{\\n502: \\t\\tSeverity:    hcl.DiagError,\\n503: \\t\\tSummary:     errInvalidValue,\\n504: \t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2\"\n12: \t\"github.com/hashicorp/hcl/v2/gohcl\"\n13: \t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty\"\n14: \t\"github.com/zclconf/go-cty/cty/convert\"\n15: \n16:
